narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to becoming a lawyer, drawing from patterns in thousands of similar paths to predict if you'd conquer the bar exam. Your story starts strong in some ways, with a solid LSAT score of 34 and a middle-family income background that shows real grit, but the model zeroed in on a few key chapters that raised concerns about the road ahead. The strongest signal came from your Year 3 decile of 7, which stood out as the biggest pull toward potential challenges in finishing law school strong—it's like the model saw that mid-pack performance late in the game as a predictor of hurdles on the bar. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 7, reinforcing a pattern of steady but not standout academic momentum from the start. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 added to that narrative, suggesting the rigorous demands of law might build on a foundation that hasn't always shone brightest. Even factors like being a full-time student, your white racial background, female gender, and that middle income wove in as subtle threads, each nudging the prediction toward a 43% chance of bar exam struggles based on how they've played out for others. It's not that any one piece doomed your application—your LSAT shows real promise—but together, they painted a picture of someone who might face an uphill battle to pass the bar on the first try. This isn't a judgment on your potential or heart; it's the model highlighting risks from data-driven stories like yours. The good news? You're in control of rewriting this story—targeting higher deciles or bolstering weak spots could shift the odds dramatically next time. You've got the drive to be full-time and chase this dream; now it's about stacking the deck in your favor. Keep pushing—you're closer than it feels. With encouragement, the admissions advisor.
